---
title: "Scientometrics in R"
subtitle: "Bibliometrics, Network Science, and Research Evaluation"
author: "R. Heller"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
site: bookdown::bookdown_site
documentclass: book
bibliography: [references.bib, packages.bib]
biblio-style: apalike
csl: style/chicago-author-date.csl
link-citations: yes
github-repo: r-heller/scientometrics-in-r
url: https://r-heller.github.io/scientometrics-in-r/
description: "A hands-on guide to scientometrics in R: data acquisition, bibliometric indicators, network analysis, text mining, and reproducible reporting."
cover-image: "images/cover.png"
---

# Welcome! {-}

```{r cover, echo=FALSE, out.width="280px", fig.align="left"}
knitr::include_graphics("images/cover.png")
```

A hands-on guide to scientometrics in R --- from raw bibliographic data to publication-ready analyses.

## About this book

*Scientometrics in R* is a working guide for researchers, librarians, research administrators, and data scientists who want to analyse scholarly communication quantitatively. It walks the full pipeline: pulling records from open databases (OpenAlex, Crossref, PubMed), computing standard indicators (h-index, MNCS, journal metrics), building co-authorship, co-citation, and keyword networks, applying text and topic models to scientific corpora, and packaging the results into reproducible reports and dashboards.

The book assumes a working knowledge of R at the tidyverse level and basic statistics. No prior scientometric experience is needed; the early chapters lay the conceptual and ethical foundations, and each later chapter starts with the question it answers and ends with a callout on responsible use grounded in the Leiden Manifesto and DORA.

All examples run against free, open data --- no Web of Science or Scopus subscription is required. Small sample datasets ship with the companion R package `scientometricsInR`; everything else is fetched live from public APIs.

## How to use this book

- **Linear or lookup.** Read front to back, or use the *Find Your Method* navigator (Chapter \@ref(find-your-method)) to jump to the chapter that answers your question.
- **Code.** Reusable helpers live in the companion package and in `R/` in the source repository; every chapter shows the full chunk it depends on.
- **Corrections.** Please file an issue at <https://github.com/r-heller/scientometrics-in-r/issues>.
- **Per-chapter PDFs.** Each chapter page has a *Download this chapter (PDF)* button at the top right.
- **Whole-book downloads.** Use the *Download* menu in the top navigation bar for PDF and EPUB.

## License

Text and figures are released under [CC BY-SA 4.0](LICENSE); source code is MIT.

## Citing this Guide

If you use this book, please cite it. The full citation, BibTeX, and RIS downloads live on the [Citing this Guide](#citing-this-guide) page.
